title: Mel Kiper's 2026 NFL Mock Draft: Expert Picks and Analysis for Round 1's Top Prospects
body:
The 2026 NFL draft is rapidly approaching, and with free agency now complete, the landscape for potential first-round picks is shifting. In response, top draft experts are revisiting their projections for the top 32 picks. ESPN's renowned draft analyst, Mel Kiper Jr., has released his latest mock draft, predicting the order in which top prospects will come off the board.

According to Kiper's updated mock draft, several key trades have altered the draft's trajectory. The Denver Broncos' deal with the Miami Dolphins, which involved wide receiver Jaylen Waddle, has had a ripple effect on the picks at Nos. 11, 13, and 30. These changes reflect the dynamic nature of the draft, where a single trade can significantly impact a team's prospects.

One of the most notable changes in Kiper's mock draft is the potential selection of quarterback Caleb Williams by the Cincinnati Bengals at No. 11. The Bengals, who are in dire need of a franchise quarterback, had previously been linked to other prospects. However, with Williams' impressive collegiate career and the team's apparent desire to bolster their offense, a quarterback pick may now be on the table. This would be a significant departure from the Bengals' traditional defensive-minded draft strategy.

Another team that has seen its draft plans altered is the Carolina Panthers. Originally projected to select defensive tackle Will Anderson Jr. at No. 13, the Panthers have shifted gears and now have their sights set on Alabama's edge rusher, Will Anderson Jr.'s counterpart, Jammie Mosley, according to sources. Anderson's impressive athleticism and pass-rushing skills make him an attractive option for a Panthers team looking to bolster its pass rush.

The Miami Dolphins, fresh off the trade with the Broncos, find themselves in an intriguing position. With the added pick at No. 30, the team may have its sights set on a top prospect who fell outside their original range. According to Kiper's mock draft, the Dolphins are now projected to select versatile wide receiver, Jahan Dotson, at No. 30. Dotson's speed, agility, and route-running skills make him an attractive option for a Dolphins team looking to bolster its receiving corps.

As the 2026 NFL draft approaches, teams are continuing to re-evaluate their needs in the wake of free agency. The ever-changing landscape of the draft is a testament to the fluid nature of the NFL's offseason. With Mel Kiper's mock draft serving as a guide, teams are now preparing for the possibility of a wide range of outcomes. As the draft approaches, fans and analysts alike will be eagerly watching to see which teams take the biggest risks and which prospects ultimately find themselves wearing new uniforms come April.
